body.title: DaemonSupervisor: add active health polling thread (5s interval) + restart.flag on thread death
body.result: start_polling() added to DaemonSupervisor: 5s poll, critical thread death → DAEMON_DEAD log + restart.flag, wired from main.py run() before loop. 1684 tests pass. Committed 366f0e52.
body.description: DaemonSupervisor exists (daemon_supervisor.py) and registers 7 threads, but has no polling loop — it only reports status on demand. Panel 11: if a worker thread dies mid-flight (e.g. embedder-warmup, consolidation-worker), nothing detects it until next status() call. Fix: add a daemon supervisor polling thread (5s interval) that calls health_fn() for each registered thread, logs CRITICAL if thread is dead, and writes restart.flag if ne-worker or consolidation-worker dies. Pair with T-main-loop-exception outer handler.
